summary.intent: Address a PR review comment on TagGroup that flagged an over-broad disabled-state fix — the reviewer wanted disabled tags to still emit observable focus/blur events for accessibility tooling, while keeping mutation and navigation blocked.
summary.outcome: Restored Focus/Blur state recording for disabled tags in the TagGroup state machine, narrowed the proptest invariant to only enforce per-item enabled status when the group is interactive, and pushed the fix as a follow-up commit to PR #713.

repo: TagGroup disabled state has two distinct layers: interaction blocking (selection, removal, arrow navigation) vs. state observation (focus/blur for AT/programmatic access) — these must be treated separately.

tag_attrs() already conditionally emits data-ars-focus-visible based on recorded focus context, so restoring disabled Focus/Blur events automatically surfaces the attribute without extra rendering logic.

Proptest state machines for TagGroup live in crates/ars-components/tests/proptest_state_machines/data_display/tag_group.rs and are run with --ignored; they require PROPTEST_CASES env var to control fuzz depth.
The project uses a co-author trailer 'Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>' on AI-assisted commits.

code.finding: The disabled event dispatch block: only the Focus arm needed to return Some(state_with_focus); Navigation, selection, deselection, toggle, and removal still return None while disabled.
The proptest invariant asserting 'focused tag is per-item enabled' was over-broad; it must only apply when the group itself is in an interactive (non-disabled) state.

workflow: Write a failing regression test before touching the implementation to confirm the exact broken behavior, then fix.
Run the full proptest suite after any state-machine invariant change — the broader fuzz coverage catches over-tightened assertions that unit tests miss.
Sequence: regression test → implementation patch → proptest → spec-conformance → fmt check → diff review → commit.

friction: The original disabled-focus fix was over-broad, requiring a review cycle to discover the AT/programmatic focus distinction — the spec wording ('disabled blocks mutation/navigation/removal') did not make the focus-observation carve-out explicit enough.
The proptest failure surface was not obvious until actually running the ignored suite; the invariant had been silently over-constrained.
open_items: Spec wording in spec/components/data-display/tag-group.md could be clarified to explicitly distinguish 'blocked interactions' from 'observable state events' for disabled tags, preventing similar review cycles.
